reviews.review_description:
First of all skelo is homophobic and owns the most unoriginal, laggy and the worst game of all time. 

The tutorial has NO correlation between itself and the real game and you have to spawn in it every time you open the game.

Almost all of your game assets are either ripped from sketchfab, or someone else, and the game has absolutely NO concurrent players.

You heard me when I called you homophobic.

Never make a game again and don’t even touch any game software until you find your balls and man up gng 😭

First of all the big one, Skelo (Game Owner) is a homophobic little kid.

Second it is very clear most of the assets are taken from Sketchfab or someone else, Also the tutorial is not very clear AND not to mention you have to play the tutorial EVERY TIME YOU OPEN THE GAME!!!!

Also the game randomly crashes + I'm pretty sure I got banned for setting my name to "Gay" which just proves the owner is homophobic.

One more thing, Anyone writing 5 star reviews is either trying to get mod or just has low standards for games and does not know who Skelo is.

Personally the monsters are not very scary and the animations just suck. Not to mention that the owner  skelo is apparently homophobic and racist. This game just sucks and the owner is problematic I would not recommend this game.
This game tries to hard to be medieval timey while having horrible updates and having the worst possible takes on maps
every asset is skidded and from sketchfab :joy:

reviews.review_title: Its pretty good but bad because of skelo
reviews.review_description: Skelo's horrific madness is a fascinating, frustrating paradox. There are moments of genuine, chilling brilliance where the atmosphere curdles with a palpable sense of dread, and the grotesque visuals sear themselves into your mind, proving there's a raw, potent talent at work. Yet, just as you're pulled into its terrifying depths, the experience is often undermined by Skelo's own hand whether through a jarring narrative misstep, a clumsily executed effect, or a descent into incoherence that feels less like artistic choice and more like a lack of control. It's this very tension that defines the experience: it's pretty good because of its audacious, unsettling vision, but ultimately bad because of Skelo's inability to fully harness the very madness he so compellingly creates.
